findings.justification: The raw $_REQUEST['args'] value is passed without any sanitization or validation directly as the argument to do_action('um_ajax_load_posts__{$hook}', $args), allowing an attacker to supply arbitrary data to any registered hook handler.
The output buffered from do_action() is echoed via die($output) without any escaping or sanitization, enabling XSS if any hook handler outputs attacker-controlled data.

The $status parameter is interpolated directly into the transient key string ("um_count_users_{$status}") without sanitization, which could allow an attacker-controlled caller to read or poison arbitrary transient cache entries.

The loose equality operator (==) is used to compare the post meta value against $value, which can produce unexpected true results due to PHP type juggling (e.g., integer 0 equals any non-numeric string).

The code assigns $_REQUEST['args'] to $args without verifying it is an array, so a string or other scalar can be passed to action hook handlers that assume an array, potentially causing unexpected behavior or PHP warnings exploitable for information disclosure.

The raw SQL query uses SELECT * on wp_posts, fetching all post fields including post_password and other sensitive columns that are not needed, unnecessarily expanding the data exposure surface in the returned result set.
